findings.signature: 8-track phase-parent coordination surface with validate.sh handoff contract
findings.notes: The 8-track decomposition (000-008) and phase transition rules are the canonical program structure. Any future program-packet can adopt this topology. Phase handoff criteria at spec.md:117-123 define cross-track dependency contracts.

findings.signature: Deferred adoption-uplift track (phase runner, edge explanation, affordance evidence, causal-trust display)
findings.notes: 005 is the only deferred top-level track (5% complete). Its children (001-phase-runner, 002-edge-explanation, 003-affordance-evidence, 004-causal-trust-display) are all deferred. Two children (005-deep-review-findings, 006-deep-research-review) are abandoned. This is the primary gap in the 026 program.

findings.signature: Migration bridge resolving 17 top-level phases to 7 themed parents with old-path resolution tables
findings.notes: Old phase paths (000-016) are NOT rewritten; resolve via bridge tables. Any new code or docs referencing old paths must consult this file. Wave 8 (2026-06-03) relocated 6 skilled-agent-orchestration packets into 026 tracks.

findings.signature: Level 3 research coordination root: 88 findings, 10 ranked recommendations, trust-axis separation, honest measurement contract
findings.notes: Key research outputs: research/research.md (canonical synthesis), research/recommendations.md (ranked decision surface), research/findings-registry.json (88 findings). The trust-axis separation (provenance, evidence status, freshness as separate fields) is a durable architectural decision (ADR R10 replacement).

findings.signature: Publish provisional honest measurement contract before any multiplier claims
findings.notes: Decision: no performance claims without the measurement contract. This gates downstream packaging work (including 005 deferred track).

findings.signature: Skill-advisor graph, scoring engine, routing engine with playbook-run remediation
findings.notes: 002-skill-advisor is in-progress. The advisor graph, scoring, and routing are live. Depends on 002 (internals) being stable before 006 (operator tooling) can reference it per phase handoff rules.

findings.signature: Concrete-token spec-folder naming policy — deferred at 0%
findings.notes: Deferred child: naming policy for spec folders. No work started. May matter if future program packets need consistent naming.

findings.signature: Memory quality, continuity gates, memory-save rewrite, runtime hardening, indexer invariants
findings.notes: 001-continuity-memory-runtime is complete. The memory-save rewrite and continuity gates are the live subsystem. Cross-links to code-graph (distinct structural surface, not a shared store).

findings.signature: Causal-graph channel routing MVP — 85% complete
findings.notes: 002-causal-graph-channel-routing in-progress at 85%. The causal-graph channel is a distinct routing surface in the memory search pipeline.

findings.signature: Local-embedding foundation with ollama/bge promotion and rerank-sidecar — 75% complete
findings.notes: 003-embedder-testing-and-architecture at 75%. Embedding consolidation (010-embedding-consolidation-hf-local-server) and stack hardening (011-embedding-stack-hardening) are in-progress children. The local-first resolveProvider() cascade (ADR-014) is the key decision.

findings.signature: Standalone code-graph package with CocoIndex decoupled, 10 sub-themes, 90% complete
findings.notes: The most complete track. Key surfaces: runtime-and-scan (004), resilience-and-advisor (005), extraction-and-isolation (006), docs-and-readmes (007), playbook-validation (010). Code graph is a structural surface distinct from memory store. CocoIndex decoupled at 95% (002-deprecate-coco-index).

findings.signature: Three-way isolation: system-code-graph extraction as standalone MCP, distinct from spec-kit-memory and spec-kit-shared
findings.notes: 006-extraction-and-isolation completed the standalone-MCP pivot. The three-way isolation (memory / code-graph / shared) is an architectural boundary to respect.

findings.signature: Runtime hook parity across Claude, Codex, Copilot, OpenCode — 85% complete
findings.notes: 001-hook-parity at 85%. Cross-runtime hook schema and wiring is the deliverable. Depends on 002-spec-kit-internals (advisor + template) being stable first.

findings.signature: Doctor command surface with dependency-safe rebuild orchestration — 90% complete
findings.notes: 002-doctor-update-orchestrator at 90%. The /doctor surface is the operator diagnostic entry point. Relocated doctor-install-alignment from skilled-agent-orchestration (context-index.md wave 8).

findings.signature: Install guides/scripts + doctor realignment for post-CocoIndex world — deferred
findings.notes: Deferred at 0%. Post-CocoIndex install scripts not yet updated. Blocked by CocoIndex decoupling (004-code-graph/002) being finalized.

findings.signature: MCP daemon lifecycle: IPC socket canonicalization, WAL checkpoint, graceful shutdown, provider dispose, watchdog, bridge liveness
findings.notes: 7 sub-phases. Phases 1,3,4 complete (socket dir, research, nondestructive build). Phases 5-7 planned (provider dispose, watchdog, bridge liveness). The daemon-reliability research (003) produced a ranked, adversarially-verified fix roadmap.

findings.signature: Root-cause map: RC-1 OOM/embedding RSS, RC-2 no watchdog, RC-3 dead-socket bridge, RC-4 build wipes dist, RC-5 /tmp socket dir
findings.notes: The RC taxonomy from the daemon-reliability research is the canonical failure-mode reference. Phases 5-7 map to RC-1 through RC-3. Phase 4 fixed RC-4. Phase 1 fixed RC-5.

findings.signature: mk-code-graph bridge restore REVERTED due to dual-writer hazard on memory DB; fix deferred to IPC-backed transport in 028
findings.notes: Critical safety decision: the bridge import fix was attempted and reverted because the bridge's startup path calls initializeDb()+sessionManager.init() against the memory DB the daemon owns — the incident the 028 research forbids. Proper fix is IPC-backed transport (028 code-index phase 3).

findings.signature: Codex hooks rewired from Claude scripts to purpose-built Codex adapters; PreCompact removed as unsupported
findings.notes: Codex hooks now emit Codex-shaped envelopes. PreCompact hook removed — the hook_system.md contract lists Codex compaction as unsupported. Applied and smoke-verified.

findings.signature: Verify-first scouted bugfix batches (3 completed) with release-readiness deep-review train
findings.notes: The scouted-bugfix-train pattern (010-scouted-bugfix-train/ with batches 001-003) is a reusable verify-first remediation approach. Wave 8 relocated batches 1-3 from skilled-agent-orchestration. Batch-4 is the next slot.

findings.signature: Clean-room license audit for external-project adoption — deferred
findings.notes: Deferred child. Blocks safe external-project adoption licensing. Low urgency until adoption track (005) is prioritized.

findings.signature: Local-first resolveProvider() cascade (ADR-014) relocated from skilled-agent-orchestration
findings.notes: 016-embedding-provider-local-first implements ADR-014. Sits with the embedding cluster (008/009/010/011). Key decision: local-first embedding provider cascade to avoid external dependency.

findings.signature: Memory store and code graph are distinct structural surfaces that cross-link, not a shared store
findings.notes: Architectural boundary: memory (003) and code-graph (004) are separate subsystems. Phase handoff rule spec.md:120 states 'code-graph is a distinct structural surface that cross-links to memory, not a shared store'. Any future work must respect this split.

findings.signature: Public preserves its split semantic/structural/continuity topology; seam-hardening contracts precede packaging
findings.notes: The v2 headline decision: do NOT replace Public's split topology with a monolithic facade. Front-load trust and freshness contracts before any packaging work. This is NFR-T01 (topology preservation).
